request.system:
You extract keyed dungeon areas from tabletop adventure module pages. The user message names the areas to extract, then interleaves each page's extracted text (each headed by a [page N] marker) with that page's image. Return one entry per named area, using exactly the canonical keys given.

Rules:
- "encounters" are the area's hostile or monstrous inhabitants with stat blocks. Ordinary townsfolk and named NPCs without a creature type are not encounters — they belong in the description. Use the creature type as printed ("goblin"), putting proper names ("Snagg") in the description. Wandering-monster tables are not keyed encounters.
- Encounter counts: put a plain number in "count_fixed" and dice notation like "2d4" in "count_dice"; anything the module says about count that fits neither (rates, conditions, ranges) goes in "count_note". Leave unused count fields null.
- "trap" describes the area's trap, null when it has none. "features" lists notable fixtures a referee should know about (altars, pools, levers, secret doors); "treasure" lists the area's treasure as printed.
- "connections": derive each connection's "direction" from the level map when the text is silent; use "unknown" only when neither the text nor the map says. For "to_key", use the connected area's canonical key when it appears in the area list; otherwise its printed label.
- "source_pages" refer to the [page N] markers in this request, never to page numbers printed on the pages.
- "confidence" is your self-assessment in [0, 1] of how faithfully you extracted that area.

parts.text:
Extract the keyed areas listed below from dungeon "Halfling Home" (id: halfling-home), level 1.
Areas to extract (canonical key | printed key | name | expected pages):
- h1 | printed key "H1" | Front Door | pages 16
- h2 | printed key "H2" | Living Room | pages 16
- h3 | printed key "H3" | Dining Room | pages 16
- h4 | printed key "H4" | Kitchen | pages 16
- h5 | printed key "H5" | Bedroom | pages 16
- h6 | printed key "H6" | Bedroom | pages 16
- h7 | printed key "H7" | Bedroom | pages 16
- h8 | printed key "H8" | Back Door | pages 17
- h9 | printed key "H9" | Tree House | pages 17
- h10 | printed key "H10" | Stables and Storage | pages 17
- h11 | printed key "H11" | Buried House | pages 17
- h12 | printed key "H12" | Living Room | pages 17
- h13 | printed key "H13" | Kitchen | pages 17
- h14 | printed key "H14" | Master Bedroom | pages 17
- h15 | printed key "H15" | Guest Room | pages 17
This level's map is on page 21.

[page 16]
The Town and Surroundings The Chaotic Caves
All of the horses and wagons belong to the
merchants inside. If rescued, the owners will
be grateful and offer a 500 gp reward, which is
most of the money they have with them. They
were moving their small household when
ambushed. 
If the PCs kill or rob the merchants, there is a
good chance they will later be recognized and
reported as bandits if they keep the wagons
and horses and remain in the area.
The horses are detailed in case they become a
target.
Bandit Leader, Human Fighter 2: AC 15, AB +2, 
#At 1 shortsword, Dam 1d6, Mv 20', Sv F2, ML 8,
XP 75
HP 9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
9 Bandits, Human Fighter 1: AC 13, AB +1, 
#At 1 shortsword, Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8,
XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
2 Dogs: AC 14, HD 1+1, #At 1 bite, Dam 1d4 + 
hold, Mv 50', Sv F1, ML 9, XP 25 ea.
HP 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
6 Riding Horses: AC 13, HD 2, #At 2 hooves, 
Dam 1d4 hoof, Mv 80' (10'), Sv F2, ML 7, 
XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 7 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
3. HALFLING HOME (See the Miscellaneous
Maps): The party comes across what was once
a Halfling farm and orchard. From all evidence,
this was abandoned decades ago. It is now
grown over with brush, weeds, and trees. The
main buildings are built like a burrow, with
only the round front door and round windows
(shown as circles on the maps) exposed. The
rest is buried underneath a dozen feet of earth.
The ceilings are uniformly 6 feet high. The
floors and walls are stone; the ceilings are
made of and held up by heavy timbers.
Cobwebs and dirt clutter all the inner rooms.
Only the windows provide light inside. The
windows are filthy from decades of neglect.
They are composed of multiple panes of glass
with wooden framework, but exact details are
left to the GM's imagination.
H1. FRONT DOOR: A large, round green door
greets visitors. The door is locked, but is old
and rotten, and can be easily knocked in.
H2. LIVING ROOM: The main living room, with a
fireplace in the south wall and a round window
about 4' in diameter in the north. There is no
furniture here. Searching the fireplace for
loose stones will reveal a small, secret niche
(about 1' squared) off to one side which
contains an old, ornate jewelry box. Inside are
100 gp, a gem worth 250 gp, and a large silver
hat pin that was once magical but is not any
longer.
The stink of death is here. A long-dead body
clad in old, rotten leather armor lies mauled in
the center of the room. It has a silver dagger
in a belt sheath, 2 vials of holy water, 1 Potion
of Healing, and a leather pouch with 3 cp.
A tentacle worm lurks in the dark, scuttling to
any sounds that occur in adjacent rooms, such
as the sound of the front door being knocked
in.
Tentacle Worm: AC 13, HD 3*, #At 6 tentacles, 
Dam paralysis, Mv 40', Sv F3, ML 9, XP 175
HP 12 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
H3. DINING ROOM: This 15 x 17 foot room has
two round windows in the north wall. The
color of the floor and shapes in the dust
indicate that tables and chairs were once
placed in the light of the windows.
H4. KITCHEN: A stone stove rests against the
north wall with a chimney going out the roof
(with a flue about 1 foot square). There are no
other furnishings. A round window about 4
feet in diameter is in the northwest corner (the
glass is broken out). A pool of slimy green
water is running down the sill of the window to
the floor (it looks exactly like a green slime
monster but is not one).
H5, H6, H7. BEDROOMS: Empty of even
furniture. Each has a round window about 4' in
diameter in the south wall (in the southwest
corner of H7).
14

[page 17]
The Chaotic Caves The Town and Surroundings
H8. BACK DOOR: A simple round door about 5'
high with old, peeling red paint. Barred from
the inside, it is old and easily knocked in.
H9. TREE HOUSE: Fire beetles have taken up
residence in this old tree house. They will
scuttle or jump down to attack. The tree
house is a simple square about 20' up from the
ground and perhaps 6' from floor to ceiling.
3 Giant Fire Beetles: AC 16, HD 1+2, #At 1 bite, 
Dam 2d4, Mv 40', Sv F1, ML 7, XP 25 ea.
HP 7 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐ 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐
H10. STABLES AND STORAGE: North of the
house is a set of double doors leading into
another mound. This is a shed. The floor is
dirt, but the wall frames are built from heavy
stone blocks and the ceiling is made of heavy
wooden timbers. The east, north, and west
walls have round windows about 3' in
diameter; these do not have glass, but do have
shutters. The one on the north wall is broken
and smeared by splattered feces (see below).
In the west end are two stalls for ponies
(empty). The east end once held carts, as well
as anything else being stored. Several piles of
what were once sacks of grain lie moldering,
covered in a harmless yellow fungus.
The floor of this dark, humid abode is covered
in stinking feces. It is currently occupied by
stirges that fly in-and-out the broken window
in the north wall. Encrusted by droppings are
3d6 gp, 4d6 sp, and 5d6 cp, plus a few bones
and bits of hair.
6 Stirges: AC 13, HD 1*, #At 1 bite, Dam 1d4 + 
1d4/round blood drain, Mv 10' Fly 60', Sv F1, 
ML 9, XP 37 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 7 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
3 ☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
H11. BURIED HOUSE: This mound of earth does
not look like the other buildings: it is grown
over with brush and weeds. Square stones
resting in loose earth near the main entrance,
though, hint that something may be buried
here. It would not take a party long to dig far
enough into the mound to reach something
interesting (maybe an hour or two). Searching
up top will reveal the small, one-foot square
chimney to the fireplace in H12. As a reminder:
all of the windows (shown as round circles)
and doors are buried. The two main doors are
boarded shut with quite a lot of lumber, as if
fear motivated the actions.
H12. LIVING ROOM: An unfurnished room with
a brick fireplace in the northwest corner. Lying
on the floor are four humanoid shapes
swaddled in cloth. These zombies will rise up
and attack if anyone enters the home.
4 Zombies: AC 12 (½ damage from blunt 
weapons, 1 point only from arrows or bolts), 
HD 2, #At 1 bludgeon, Dam 1d8, Mv 20', Sv F2, 
ML 12, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
H13. KITCHEN: This room is crammed with two
cozy chairs, a couch, a table, six wooden
chairs, cupboards, a stove, and other
furnishings.
H14. MASTER BEDROOM: Besides a bed and
dresser, this room features a writing table.
Sitting behind it is a figure in tattered clothes,
its exposed features putrid and ragged from
wounds and age. This zombie will lift a flail (as
war hammer) up and attack intruders, growling
in hatred of anything living.
Zombie: AC 12 (½ damage from blunt weapons, 
1 point only from arrows or bolts), HD 2, #At 1 
flail, Dam 1d8, Mv 20', Sv F2, ML 12, XP 75
HP 16 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐
Lying on the desk are several sheets of old
paper scrawled with many different and
poorly-composed poems to "Kylenne My Lady
Love" mixed in with the name "Tyrok the
Indefatigable". The writer was obviously very
disturbed and quite evil. A necklace with gems
worth 500 gp lies on the paper and is
mentioned as a "gift to my love." The zombie
has a massive hole in its back as if it died from
a particularly brutal sword wound.
H15. GUEST ROOM: A bed, table, small dresser,
and few other furnishings are still here.
4. RESCUE: The PCs hear the squealing of an
angry hog and see a fleeing man leap onto
15

[page 21]
The Chaotic Caves The Town and Surroundings
Miscellaneous Maps
19

areas.description: A large, round green front door to the halfling burrow. It is locked, but old and rotten, and can be easily knocked in.
features: locked but rotten and easy to knock in

areas.description: The main living room. A fireplace is set in the south wall and a round window about 4 feet in diameter is in the north wall. There is no furniture. The stink of death fills the room. A long-dead mauled body in rotten leather armor lies in the center; it carries a silver dagger in a belt sheath, 2 vials of holy water, 1 Potion of Healing, and a leather pouch with 3 cp. A tentacle worm lurks in the dark and scuttles to sounds in adjacent rooms, such as the front door being knocked in.
treasure: An old ornate jewelry box hidden in a small secret niche in the fireplace contains 100 gp, a gem worth 250 gp, and a large silver hat pin that was once magical but is not any longer.
On the long-dead body: a silver dagger, 2 vials of holy water, 1 Potion of Healing, and a leather pouch with 3 cp.
features: small secret niche in fireplace found by searching for loose stones
long-dead mauled body in center of room

areas.description: A 15 x 17 foot dining room with two round windows in the north wall. The color of the floor and shapes in the dust indicate that tables and chairs were once placed in the light of the windows.
features: two round windows in north wall
dust marks showing where tables and chairs once stood

areas.description: A kitchen with a stone stove against the north wall and a chimney going out the roof with a flue about 1 foot square. There are no other furnishings. A round window about 4 feet in diameter is in the northwest corner; the glass is broken out. A pool of slimy green water runs down the sill of the window to the floor. It looks exactly like a green slime monster but is not one.
features: round window in northwest corner with broken glass
pool of slimy green water that resembles green slime but is harmless

areas.description: An empty bedroom with no furniture. It has a round window about 4 feet in diameter in the south wall.
features: round window about 4 feet in diameter in south wall

areas.description: An empty bedroom with no furniture. It has a round window about 4 feet in diameter in the south wall.
features: round window about 4 feet in diameter in south wall

areas.description: An empty bedroom with no furniture. It has a round window about 4 feet in diameter in the southwest corner.
features: round window about 4 feet in diameter in southwest corner

areas.description: A simple round back door about 5 feet high with old, peeling red paint. It is barred from the inside, old, and easily knocked in.
features: simple round door about 5 feet high
old and easy to knock in

areas.description: An old tree house occupied by fire beetles. They will scuttle or jump down to attack. The tree house is a simple square about 20 feet up from the ground and perhaps 6 feet from floor to ceiling.

A shed north of the house entered by double doors into another mound. The floor is dirt, the wall frames are heavy stone blocks, and the ceiling is made of heavy wooden timbers. Round windows about 3 feet in diameter are in the east, north, and west walls; these have shutters but no glass. The north window is broken and smeared with splattered feces. Two empty pony stalls stand in the west end. The east end once held carts and storage. Several piles of what were once sacks of grain lie moldering, covered in a harmless yellow fungus. The dark, humid floor is covered in stinking feces. Stirges fly in and out through the broken north window.
treasure: Encrusted by droppings are 3d6 gp, 4d6 sp, and 5d6 cp.
features: round shuttered windows in east, north, and west walls
broken north window smeared with feces
two empty pony stalls in west end
moldering grain sacks covered in harmless yellow fungus

areas.description: A mound of earth unlike the other buildings, overgrown with brush and weeds. Square stones resting in loose earth near the main entrance hint that something is buried here. Digging into the mound for an hour or two reaches the buried house. Searching on top reveals the small one-foot-square chimney to the fireplace in H12. All windows and doors are buried. The two main doors are boarded shut with a great deal of lumber, apparently out of fear.
features: earth mound overgrown with brush and weeds
square stones near main entrance hint at burial
digging for an hour or two reaches the house
small one-foot-square chimney visible on top leading to H12 fireplace
two main doors heavily boarded shut

areas.description: An unfurnished living room with a brick fireplace in the northwest corner. Four humanoid shapes swaddled in cloth lie on the floor; they are zombies that rise and attack if anyone enters the home.
features: four cloth-swaddled humanoid shapes on floor

areas.description: A kitchen crammed with two cozy chairs, a couch, a table, six wooden chairs, cupboards, a stove, and other furnishings.

The master bedroom. Besides a bed and dresser, it features a writing table. Sitting behind it is a figure in tattered clothes, with exposed features putrid and ragged from wounds and age. This zombie attacks intruders with a flail (as war hammer), growling in hatred of anything living. On the desk are several sheets of old paper scrawled with many different and poorly composed poems to "Kylenne My Lady Love" mixed with the name "Tyrok the Indefatigable." The writer was obviously very disturbed and quite evil. The zombie has a massive hole in its back as if it died from a particularly brutal sword wound.
treasure: A necklace with gems worth 500 gp lies on the writing desk.
features: old papers with poems to "Kylenne My Lady Love" and the name "Tyrok the Indefatigable"
zombie's back has a massive hole from a brutal sword wound

areas.description: A guest room with a bed, table, small dresser, and a few other furnishings still present.
